title: Week of April 6, 2026
summary: This week focused on major platform reliability improvements, with automatic cleanup of outdated configurations and missing dependencies that previously caused session startup issues. The platform now automatically detects and fixes common setup problems, while new compliance checks ensure data flows work consistently across all your revenue operations workflows.

entries.headline: Enhanced data flow validation prevents workflow interruptions
details: All data transfers now verify proper formatting before processing
Advisory workflows can no longer accidentally block other operations
Faster processing when running unrelated automation tasks

entries.headline: Automatic cleanup removes outdated files and improves performance
details: Removes old temporary files that slow down your workspace
Clears abandoned session locks that prevent new workflows from starting
Alerts you when memory usage approaches limits

entries.headline: Platform automatically removes broken automation components
details: Works across Windows, Mac, and Linux systems
Detects and removes any marketplace integration that stops working
Prevents failed automations from affecting other workflows

entries.headline: Salesforce integration prevents common field and profile errors
details: Stops invisible field additions when Dynamic Forms are active
Automatically assigns new record types to admin profiles
Converts workflow blocking errors into helpful suggestions

entries.headline: Missing dependencies install automatically at startup
details: Platform detects and installs missing components during session start
Shows clear error messages when installations fail
Provides specific fix commands for manual troubleshooting
